Attributes.Description: The full official name of the league or competition.
The name of the country where the league is based or primarily takes place.
The full name of the player.
The date of birth of the player, typically in YYYY-MM-DD format.
The height of the player, usually measured in centimeters.
The weight of the player, typically measured in kilograms.
The date when the player's attributes were recorded or updated.
The overall performance rating of the player, usually on a fixed scale (e.g., 0–100).
The predicted peak performance rating the player can achieve in the future.
The foot the player prefers to use — typically 'left' or 'right'.
The player's tendency to contribute to offensive play, described as 'low', 'medium', or 'high'.
The player's tendency to contribute to defensive actions, described as 'low', 'medium', or 'high'.
The player’s ability to deliver accurate crosses from wide areas.
The player’s ability to score goals from shooting situations.
The player’s accuracy and skill in heading the ball.
The accuracy and effectiveness of the player's short-distance passes.
The technique and accuracy of the player's volleyed shots.
The player’s skill in moving past opponents while controlling the ball.
The ability to bend or curve the ball, typically useful for free kicks and crosses.
The player's precision when taking direct free kicks.
The player's ability to accurately pass the ball over longer distances.
The player's skill in receiving and maintaining control of the ball.
How quickly the player can reach top speed from a standing position.
The top running speed the player can achieve.
The player’s quickness and ease of movement, especially while changing direction.
The player’s speed and quality of responses to game situations.
The player’s ability to remain steady while dribbling or under pressure.
The power behind the player’s shots.
The vertical leap capability of the player.
The player’s ability to sustain high performance over the course of a match.
The player’s physical power and ability to hold off opponents.
The accuracy and effectiveness of shots taken from distance.
The player’s assertiveness and intensity in contests for the ball.
The player’s skill in anticipating and intercepting passes.
The offensive player's sense of positioning to receive passes or score goals.
The player’s ability to spot opportunities and make smart passes.
The player’s ability and accuracy in taking penalty kicks.
The defensive player's ability to track and limit opposing attackers.
The player’s ability to dispossess opponents while standing.
The effectiveness of the player’s sliding challenges.
The goalkeeper's ability to make diving saves.
The goalkeeper’s skill in catching or controlling the ball after a save.
The goalkeeper's ability to accurately and powerfully kick the ball.
The goalkeeper’s sense of positioning to effectively guard the goal.
The goalkeeper’s speed of reaction to shots and deflections.
The full name of the player to whom these attributes belong.
The full official name of the team.
An abbreviated or commonly used short name for the team.
The date when the team's attributes were recorded or updated.
A numeric value representing how quickly the team transitions from defense to attack.
The categorical label (e.g., 'Slow', 'Balanced', 'Fast') representing the build-up speed.
A numeric value indicating the directness or safety of passing in build-up play.
The categorical label (e.g., 'Short', 'Mixed', 'Long') describing the build-up passing strategy.
The team's general positioning strategy during build-up play (e.g., 'Organized' or 'Free Form').
A numeric value indicating how much focus is placed on passing during chance creation.
The categorical label (e.g., 'Safe', 'Normal', 'Risky') describing the passing style in attacking play.
A numeric value indicating the frequency and accuracy of crosses during offensive plays.
The categorical description of the team’s crossing strategy (e.g., 'Rarely', 'Often').
A numeric value indicating the team’s tendency to shoot or take chances.
The categorical description of the team’s shooting behavior (e.g., 'Conservative', 'Aggressive').
The team’s attacking positioning style, such as 'Organized' or 'Free Form'.
A numeric value showing how high or intense the team presses defensively.
The categorical label (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High') describing the level of defensive pressure.
A numeric value representing how aggressively the team defends.
The categorical description of the team's defensive aggression (e.g., 'Contain', 'Press').
A numeric value indicating how wide the team sets up when defending.
The categorical label (e.g., 'Narrow', 'Balanced', 'Wide') describing the defensive width.
The type of defensive line used by the team, such as 'Cover' or 'Offside Trap'.
The full official name of the team to which these attributes belong.
